coredata.dc:title: Synthesis of Green fluorescent Nitrogen doped Vitis vinifera derived Carbon dots and their in-vitro antimicrobial studies
coredata.dc:creator: Rajapandi, Subramani
Pandeeswaran, M.
Jesuraj, Dominic
Kousalya, G.N.
coredata.dc:description: The synthesised NCDs were derived from Vitis vinifera boll and it's prepared in one pot hydrothermal process. The V.vinifera derived NCDs were as exhibit the emission is (λex = 380 nm and λem = 460 nm) green fluorescence. The synthesized NCDs was preliminarily characterised by UV–vis spectroscopy, it shows the two peaks at 277 nm and 322 nm. The FT-IR and high resolution XPS was confirmed by their functional group of NCDs. The Raman spectroscopy and XRD also confirms the NCDs are graphitic in nature. Finally NCDs were applied in vitro antimicrobial study in gram positive and negative bacteria. Among the results of antimicrobial activity is shown in Minimum inhibitory concentration of the NCDs is 25 to 100 µg mL−1 respectively in different bacteria. The highest concentration of the100µg mL−1 was performed in Klebsiella Pneumoniae is zone of inhibition is 14.8 ± 1.12.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Vitis vinifera
N-CDs
Grampositive and negative bacteria
Anti-microbial viability assay
Streptomycin

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Chemicals and synthesis of nitrogen doped carbon dots
Bio-applications of V.Vinifera derived NCDs as an anti-microbial activity

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Scheme of the synthesis of NCDs from V.Vinifera.
UV–Vis Spectrum of the N-Doped C-dot (Inset: CDs in under normal light and UV-light).
Photoluminescence spectra of NCDs at different excitation wavelengths (a) From 310 nm–360 nm, (b) From 360 nm–430 nm.
(a) HR-TEM image of the NCDs in10 nm scale (Inset-SAED Pattern of NCDs), (b) EDS spectrum of NCDs.
Raman Spectroscopy of the V.Vinifera NCDs.
(a) XPS Survey scans of V.Vinifera NCDs, (b) C1s, (c) N1s, (d) O1s.

V.Vinifera NCDs involved in against human pathogens of (a)Streptococcus Pyogenes [SP](MTCC442), BbStaphylococcus Aureus [SA](MTCC10787), (c)Klebsiella Pneumoniae [KP](MTCC432), (d) Shigella Flexneri [SF](MTCC1457) in different concentrations in 25 µg, 50 µg, 75 µg, 100 µg and Streptomycin sulfate as positive control in anti-microbial agent.
Various concentration of V.Vinifera NCDs against human pathogens. (Well size 6 mm) (Values are mean of three replicates ± are the standard error).

head.ce:title: Synthesis of Green fluorescent Nitrogen doped Vitis vinifera derived Carbon dots and their in-vitro antimicrobial studies

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: PG and Research Department of Chemistry, GTN Arts College (Autonomous), Dindigul, Tamil Nadu 624005, India

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: PG and Research Department of Chemistry

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: The Gandhigram Rural Institute – Deemed to be University

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: bDepartment of Chemistry, The Gandhigram Rural Institute – Deemed to be University, Gandhigram, Tamil Nadu, India

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: The synthesised NCDs were derived from Vitis vinifera boll and it's prepared in one pot hydrothermal process. The V.vinifera derived NCDs were as exhibit the emission is (λex = 380 nm and λem = 460 nm) green fluorescence. The synthesized NCDs was preliminarily characterised by UV–vis spectroscopy, it shows the two peaks at 277 nm and 322 nm. The FT-IR and high resolution XPS was confirmed by their functional group of NCDs. The Raman spectroscopy and XRD also confirms the NCDs are graphitic in nature. Finally NCDs were applied in vitro antimicrobial study in gram positive and negative bacteria. Among the results of antimicrobial activity is shown in Minimum inhibitory concentration of the NCDs is 25 to 100 µg mL−1 respectively in different bacteria. The highest concentration of the100µg mL−1 was performed in Klebsiella Pneumoniae is zone of inhibition is 14.8 ± 1.12.

ce:list-item.ce:para: Data will be made available on request.

ce:section.ce:para: In this two decade human beings mainly affected by bacterial infections threads to seriously a human lives [1]. Researchers to develop the innovative effect full medicine in these decades. The antibiotic drug used as a treatment of bacterial infections, the anti bacterial drugs mainly cures the infected areas. The various antibiotics may cause the side effects and drug resistant issues [2]. The nanomaterials also cure the microbial infections, the various nano materials like anti-microbial polymers, metal nanomaterials, and carbon nanomaterials also [3]. We have used as an alternative medicine of antibiotics, recent days the evolving carbon dots and nano clusters are used and combat the bacterial infections because it is biocompatible and proactive and its ultra-small sizes [4]. In this recent days especially researchers have been find out the carbon dots excellent applications like anti microbial activities, it can be stronger than many traditional antiinfectious drugs [5,6]. Among the carbon dots which are carbon nanoparticles consider as an antimicrobial agent for its good bio compatibility and emerged microbial viability assays, it is stronger than ancient anti infectional drugs [7]. The ultra small anti-microbial suffer poor stability due to their large oxidised surface areas. The antibacterial agent is combating bacterial infections for the duration of long time [8,9].

Carbon dots are a new class of the nano particles and the size is less than 10 nm, Xu etal invented by the CD in 2004 [10]. The carbon dots have highly fluorescent material and it is biocompatible nanomaterials. The ultra low particle size of the carbon dots has interesting properties such as bright and luminescent properties [11]. The carbon dots is an unique material, non toxic, good biocompatible nature, water soluble in nature and high stability, good optical properties and their applications like catalytic, bio imaging, cellular imaging, drug delivery and sensor based applications [12,13]. Additional benefits of carbon dots include their advantageous optical properties, appealing catalytic performance, chemical inertness, ultra small size, environmental friendliness, and ease of preparation. These advantages give them application-specific capabilities in catalysis, optoelectronic devices, lubrication, and other fields [14]. The important factor of the carbon dots seems it is more environmental friendly nature and safe for biological users. The biocompatible nature of the carbon dots derived from fruits, flowers and leaves etc. In this carbon dots doped us nitrogen, sulfur, phosphorus and boron etc., [15,16]. In this nitrogen doped carbon dots shows the highly luminescent property and good bio compatible in nature. The overwhelming majority of carbonaceous materials have been used to produce CDs, including glucose, citric acid, graphene oxide (GO), CNTs, graphite, coal, leaves, hair, grass, durian, petroleum coke, and even household garbage [17–20].

In this work describes, Vitis vinifera (Indian grapes) boll derived carbon dots in hydrothermal method. Vitaceae family Vitis vinifera also it is called as ‘Grapes’. The Vitis vinifera has a lot of medicinal values to treat as diseases like anti inflammatory, carcinogenic, anti-oxidant, anti-diabetic, anti-neoplastic and controller of blood pressure and insulin secretor [21]. The Vitis vinifera having a more water content carbohydrates proteins and fat contents it additionally the ingredients of significant amount of potassium, vitamin C, vitamin A and also a tiny amount of calcium and phosphorus [22]. The Vitis vinifera derived carbon dot as a source of the material, we have used and it is applied to anti microbial agent in different human pathogens. We have been characterised in UV–vis spectroscopy, FTIR spectroscopy, high resolution TEM, XRD, EDS spectrum, Raman spectroscopy and the high resolution of X-Ray photo electron spectroscopy we have been characterised derived carbon dots mainly used as anti microbial agent were treated with grampositive bacteria of Streptococcus Pyogenes [SP], Staphylococcus Aureus [SA] and gramnegative bacteria of the Klebsiella Pneumoniae [KP], Shigella Flexneri [SF] antibacterial were as involved in-vitro study of disk diffusion method, our carbon dot work against the human pathogens. The nitrogen doped carbon dots inhibit the bacteria, the zone of inhibition was shows the results.

The Vitis vinifera Boll was handpicked by the Chinnalapatty area agricultural land in Dindigul District Tamilnadu, India. The Urea was purchased from Sigma Aldrich and Human pathogens (1. S. pyogenes, 2. S. aureus, 3. K. pneumoniae, 4. S.flexneri) were tested by the Biotechnology laboratory of Gandhigram University.

After washing the boll of V.Vinifera the seeds were removed and grinded by the commercial mixer grinder. The aqueous extract was collected; filtered and green color extract of the V.Vinifera boll was used as the source material of Carbon dot.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Chemicals and synthesis of nitrogen doped carbon dots
ce:section.ce:para: In this hydrothermal method is used in this synthesis, 100 mL Teflon coated cylindrical auto-clave was used, already prepared V. Vinifera boll extract 50 mL, Urea solution 5 mL were added in appropriate proportion. Further, the autoclave placed in hot air oven and temperature was fixed 200 °C in 8 h. After the reaction time auto-clave was taken from the hot air oven. The yield product of the carbon dot solution was observed as black brownish in color. The synthesised NCDs solution filtered in Whatman filter paper and centrifuges the large particles. The solution was then treated to dialysis against deionised (DI) water using a dialysis membrane for 48 h before to usage in order to eliminate any unreacted organic moieties. The final NCDs were stored in 5 °C for further characterization process.

Preliminary characterization of the UV–Vis absorbance and photoluminescence were taken of the NCDs were assessed using JASCO V-750 spectrophotometer and JASCO-8500 spectro-fluorometer respectively. The X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) instrument is PHI 5000 Versa Probe III; instrument was used at a scan rate of 20 eV for One hour. An XRD pattern was recorded by using the Analytical Xpert diffractometer. The size of NCDs were mapped by HR-TEM instrument, diluted solution of NCDs were analyzed. Jasco FT-I460 plus spectrophotometer were used as identified by the functional group of the NCDs. Energy Dispersive Spectrum data was observed by BRUKER d-12,489 instrument. The Raman spectra were observed by STR 500 nm focal length laser Raman spectrometer was utilized.

The UV–Visible absorption spectra of V.Vinifera NCDs are shown in Fig. 2 . From the UV–Vis spectrum, we can observe two peaks at 277 nm and 322 nm. The major peaks of 277 nm, 322 nm are also vicinity in nature and both the peaks having almost same absorbance value. The NCDs containing bonding and non-bonding electronic transitions, it was detected by the UV–Vis spectrum. These two peaks indicate the π-π* and n-π* transitions, it was associated with the C=C and carbonyl/hydroxyl groups respectively [23].

Further we have characterized the NCDs by fluorescence spectral technique. Fluorescence spectra of NCDs with different excitation wavelength are shown in Fig. 3 a. and b. The emission maximum was observed at 452 nm with an excitation wavelength at 310 nm (Fig. 3a). When we increase the wavelength from 320 nm to 350 nm, the emission intensity was increased and attained the emission maximum with red shift (Fig. 3a). Interestingly, highest emission maximum was obtained at 452 nm with an excitation wavelength 360 nm (Fig. 3a). Whereas, the increased excitation wavelength from 370 nm to 430 nm, the emission intensity was decreased and attained emission maximum with red shift (Fig. 3b). Hence we have fixed 360 nm as an excitation wavelength of NCDs for further studies. Moreover, NCDs were shows brown color under day light and Green emission under UV-light. This excitation dependant wavelength is perfectly matched with the previous reports [24].

The Fig. 4 a. Contains the High Resolution-Transmission Electron Microscope image of N- CDs and confirmed the presence of Carbon dots. The average size of the particle in diameter range is 6.6nm±0.17 nm and the shape of the particles is spherical in nature and the data was relates to the phase of the graphitic carbon (002) and (100) planes. In Fig. 4a. is a SAED pattern of NCDs, in this pattern confirms by the graphitic nature of NCDs [25].

The Fig. 4b. shows the Energy Dispersive Spectrum (EDS) of V.Vinifera NCDs correlative technique and supportive information of HR-TEM analysis. EDS has detected the quantitative measurement of elemental analysis. The V.Vinifera NCDs were the composition of the Carbon, Nitrogen, and Oxygen. Quantity of C (61.97%), N (5.34%), and O (32.69%) were also confirmed. The above EDS data was confirmed by presence of above mentioned elements on the surface of NCDs.

The V.Vinifera NCDs XRD pattern Fig. 5 shows the very intensive broad peak appeared in the angle of 29.98° (High intensity) and 41.12° (Low intensity), it relates to C (002) & C (100) plane respectively. From the Bragg's equation [26],
(1)
d
=
n λ
/
2 sin θ
d-spacing value of V.Vinifera NCDs were calculated. The d-spacing value of NCDs was calculated to be approximately 0.255 nm and 0.187 nm for C (002) and C (100) planes, respectively.

The FT-IR spectroscopy of prepared NCDs graph Fig. 6 . Shows the absorption bands at 3412, 3254, 1640, 1367 cm−1, it bring out the presence of functional groups on NCDs. The above peaks were confirms the presence of OH/NH, -C=C, -C=N functional groups correspondingly. In 3412 cm−1has shown a broadened spectrum reveals the presence of Hydroxyl and amine functional groups. Thus FT-IR spectrum confirms the presence of high concentration Nitrogen rich Carbon dots (NCDs).

Raman spectroscopy is important tool for characterization of carbon based nanomaterial. Fig. 7 shows the two prominent bands in Raman Spectrum such as d-band and G-band as 1375 cm−1 and 1589 cm−1, it corresponds to the sp3 defects (D band) and sp2 carbon (G band) in the synthesized NCDs [27]. The graphitic nature of the carbon materials should have the intensity ratio between D and G bands (ID/IG) to be less than 1 and the calculated ID/IG ratio of the prepared NCDs has found to be 0.865. The graphitized carbon was less defective in nature [28].

The X-ray Photoelectron spectrum of V.Vinifera NCDs was observed the three elemental analyses like Carbon (C1s), Oxygen (O1s), and Nitrogen (N1s). XPS surveys scan Fig. 8 a was showed in three peaks in the region of 285.60 eV, 399.20 eV and 533.60 eV indicates the Carbon, Nitrogen and Oxygen elements. The Fig. 8b deconvoluted C1s spectrum shows three peaks 286.32 eV, 287.5 eV, 288.07 eV, its correlates to CC, CC, CH groups correspondingly [29,30]. Fig. 8c. Deconvoluted N1s spectrum shows two peaks 397.56 eV, 396.69 eV, 402.86 eV, 405.87 eV its correlates to Pyridinic N, CN, Graphitic-Nitrogen and Oxidised-Nitrogen it corresponding to IR and EDS, it's confirmed by doping of Nitrogen [31,32]. Fig. 8d. is a deconvoluted spectrum of O1s shows two peaks 535.50 eV, 534.64 eV it correlates to C=O, CO-C groups correspondingly [33,34]. The XPS scan elemental quantitative analysis shows Carbon is 65.30%, Nitrogen 5.5%, Oxygen 29.2% thus it confirms the Nitrogen doping of the CDs.

The V.Vinifera derived NCDs implicated through the polymerization, aromatization and nucleation and growth process. The preeminent stage of inter and intra molecular dehydration in the presence of V.Vinifera extract at above 100 °C, may lead to form the larger polymeric particles [35].

ce:section.ce:section-title: Bio-applications of V.Vinifera derived NCDs as an anti-microbial activity
ce:section.ce:para: Every human being facing microbial infections, it causes like food bacteria's, water contamination, death cells [36] were as produce the fungus and microbes Human pathogens of the Fig. 9 a). Streptococcus Pyogenes [SP], b) Staphylococcus Aureus [SA], c) Klebsiella Pneumoniae [KP], d) Shigella Flexneri [SF] were taken as experimental in vitro study. The synthesised NCDs were as involved in human pathogens in different concentrations as streptomycin used as a control antibiotic. The infectious bacteria's are classified in to two types, grampositive and another one is gramnegative bacteria. The grampositive bacteria of the SP, SA and gramnegative bacteria of KP, SF. In this antimicrobial assay experiment shows, V.Vinifera derived NCDs effectively inhibit the microbial growth of grampositive and gramnegative. The NCDs were as embedded in different concentrations of 25 µg, 50 µg, 75 µg and 100 µg mL−1 at 38 °c in 48 h of the time period in LB agar disk and the bacterial inhibition decreased in 100% to 0% with increasing a different concentration of 25 µg to 100 µg. The experimental data image indicates the streptomycin antimicrobial resistant viability was decreased from 100% to 0% increasing in concentrations NCDs. This result indicates (Table 1 ) concentration of the NCDs was increased in gradually, in this grampositive and gramnegative pathogens fully inhibited in concentration of 100 µg mL−1 .

This work has confirmed the successful formation of V. Vinifera derived NCDs by hydrothermal method. The Elemental composition and surface functional groups were identified by XPS and FT-IR technique. The Carbon dot formation was confirmed by HR-TEM analysis and average size was found to be 6.6 nm and it has spherical in shape. The Fluorescent excitation spectrum shows peak at 360 nm and emission is 452 nm. The Raman spectral studies show the intensity (ID/IG) ratio as 0.865, all these studies confirms the graphitic nature of V.Vinifera derived NCDs. The EDS spectrum confirms the quantity of the elemental composition in the ratio of C-61.97%, N-5.34%, and O-32.69%. The V.Vinifera derived NCDs were found in anti-microbial activity against from the human pathogens. Finally, our N CDs show excellent antimicrobial activity in 100 µg mL−1.

Subramani Rajapandi: Software, Conceptualization, Methodology, Writing – original draft. M. Pandeeswaran: Visualization. Dominic Jesuraj: Data curation. G.N. Kousalya: Supervision, Writing – review & editing.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The author declares the, no competing financial interest.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Synthesis, characterization, and photocatalytic degradation of Rhodamine B dye under sunlight irradiation of porous titanosilicate (TS)/bismuth vanadate (BiVO4) nanocomposite hybrid catalyst
